item.title: spring = salad time !!! A lovely Mediterranean inspired pasta sa...
item.description:
View on Instagram


spring = salad time !!! A lovely Mediterranean inspired pasta salad! (I’m sorry to all the Kevin’s out there)Ingredients: -8oz casarecce (or your fav pasta) -chopped persian cucumber, red bell pepper, scallions, sun dried tomatoes (drained), mixed pitted olives -feta and mozzarella balls (because they’re fun!!) -chickpeas (drained and rinsed) -fresh mint and oregano -za’at seasoning -drizzle of the olive oil -dressing: mayo, lemon, harissa, salt, grated garlic Recipe:-Cook pasta according to the package. Combine with other ingredients and toss with za’atar seasoning and olive oil. -Measure dressing with your heart and mix together!! Add to bowl and toss again! Enjoy :’) **I got this recipe from delish.com, they have like a hundred pasta salad recipes and I love making them for a quick lunch!

item.title: I’m always in the mood for some tacos !! Recipe below :) @chos...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I’m always in the mood for some tacos !! Recipe below :) @chosenfoods #ChosenPartner Ingredients: -4-5 chicken thighs -chicken marinade: 1/4cup @chosenfoods classic mayo, 1 chipotle in adobo (minced) and 2tsp of its sauce, 1tsp salt, 1/4tsp paprika, 1/4tsp cumin, 3 grated garlic cloves, 1 lime juiced -flour or corn tortillas -verde salsa: 6-7 tomatillos, 1/2 white onion, 3 garlic cloves, 1 jalapeño, 1 serrano pepper, 1/2 cup cilantro, 1 lime juiced, 1tbsp chosen foods avocado oil, salt -toppings: diced onions, sliced radish, and cilantro Recipe: -mix together marinade ingredients and add to chicken. Massage and let rest for 30 minutes-4hours -preheat oven to 450F -remove tomatillo husks, wash well, and dry. Add to a parchment lined sheet pan with 1/4 white onion, 3 garlic cloves (you can leave the skin on), and peppers. spray with @chosenfoods avocado oil spray and sprinkle with salt. -bake for 15 min or until blistered and brown-add tomatillos, onion, peppers (optional: remove seeds to make it less spicy), garlic (remove skins), cilantro, lime, 1/4 raw white onion, and salt to a food processor. Pulse and taste, adjust to your liking. Put in the fridge -heat a large cast iron on medium-high heat. Cook chicken 5-8 min each side until internal temp is 165F, let rest. chop into bite sized -heat tortillas, add cubed chicken, diced onion, verde salsa, radish slices, and cilantro. ENJOY :)

item.title: it’s egg salad sandwich season !!! ✨🕺🏻🪩 recipe belo...
item.description:
View on Instagram


it’s egg salad sandwich season !!! ✨🕺🏻🪩 recipe below Ingredients: -4-6 eggs -your favorite bread (I recommend getting a soft white bread) -2-3tbsp mayo -dijon mustard @mailleus -DILL pickles or DILL relish (I hate sweet pickles can you tell? Lmao) @grillospickles -fresh dill -1 small shallot -salt/pepper Recipe: -in a medium sized pot bring water to a boil (med-high heat). Add eggs, let the water continue to boil/simmer and cook them for 9 minutes. Remove and add to an ice bath. I did one egg for 6 minutes so it’ll be extra runny! -mince the shallot and pickles. Chop up the dill -once the eggs are fully cooled, start peeling them. I tap the top and bottom and gently roll on the counter. The shell should easily come off! -add eggs to a bowl and mash with a fork. Fold in 2-3tbsp of Mayo, 1tsp Dijon mustard, 1/2tsp fresh dill, 1tsp pickle juice, shallots, pickles, salt and pepper. Taste and adjust to your liking!! I just eyeball this part so that’s rough estimates -turn it into a sandwich and enjoy!! I cut the 6min egg in half, placed it face down on the bread and covered it with the egg salad. :)

View on Instagram

item.title: my 36 hour ELECTRIC rafting camping experience!!! Details below ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


my 36 hour ELECTRIC rafting camping experience!!! Details below :)The most perfect rafting experience with @navtec_expeditions_moab down the Colorado river! we all met up at 8am and had a super cruisy ride to camp (seriously barely any rapids). Brian let me be his sous-chef for the night which was SO FUN. I learned so many cooking camping tips 🥲 It honestly felt like glamping. We all decided to sleep under the stars which was my first real experience of that and 10/10 recommend !! Then the next day we went down some gnarly rapids (at least for me). I’m shocked I volunteered to go in the R2 but so glad I did. Somehow I didn’t fall out which was also shocking hahahah *major thanks to Brian and his team for being so so kind and helping us with EVERYTHING *rafting filmed on my @gopro MAX (helmet mount) *the trail stars are filmed on my HERO13 *shoutout to all my new besties for help filming my cooking video (I’m looking at you @ossysends and @marissameizz)#moabmadness2025

item.title: Free falling 500 feet is the wildest thing I’ve ever done 😭...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Free falling 500 feet is the wildest thing I’ve ever done 😭 I’m not sure whether I passed out or not, but it looks like I did. I remember screaming with my eyes closed, and then opening them, watching the ground get significantly closer to me. I felt my heart rate drastically drop and thought “well.. this is it.” And then I fully let go… of everything. My memory is blank from the moment I gave into mental submission until I checked for pee (shocked there was none). The camera dropped in my lap too fast to see my facial reaction when it happened (very unfortunate) so not sure what ~really~ went down. Either way such a cool feeling and experience! 10/10 recommend giving this a try !! Thank you @ropeswingmoab and @visitutah @discovermoab for this opportunity!!! Core memory for sure 🤣🤣 Filmed on my @gopro 13 and GoPro MAX! Shoutout to @pshepfpv for the FPV footage and my friends for filming on the side 🫶🏼#moab #gopro #ropeswing #moabropeswing #moabmadness2025 #gopro #gopropartner

item.title: The loaded fries from my dreams !! Recipe below :) #ChosenPartne...
item.description:
View on Instagram


The loaded fries from my dreams !! Recipe below :) #ChosenPartner @chosenfoods Ingredients: -Sirloin steak -2-4 russets potatoes (depending on how many fries you want)-Garlic aoili: @chosenfoods avocado oil mayo, grated garlic, lemon, salt, pepper -cheese sauce: 1tbsp flour, 1tbsp butter, 1 cup milk, 1 cup shredded white cheddar, 1/3cup grated Parmesan, grated garlic, salt -side salad: mixed greens, shallots, quartered cherry tomatoes, lemon honey vinaigrette -lemon honey vinaigrette: lemon juice, @chosenfoods avocado oil, dijon mustard, honey, salt **Optional toppings: parmesan, pepper, green onions, jalapeños Recipe: -cut potatoes into even strips. Soak in ice water for at least 30 minutes. Dry completely and heat @chosenfoods avocado oil to 325F. Add fries until soften and slightly golden (5ish min), remove. Let fries fully cool. Heat oil to 375F. Add the fries back until a deep golden (5-7min), remove and top with parmesan and salt. **do this in batches! Don’t overcrowd the pot. -cook the seasoned steak to your liking :) -cheese sauce: on med heat add butter and flour. Whisk continuously until it starts to brown. Slowly add milk and continue whisking. Turn off heat and whisk in grated garlic, and the shredded cheese. taste and adjust salt, and garlic amounts -garlic aoili: measure with your heart!! Start with 1/3cup mayo, 1/2 lemon juice, and 1 garlic clove. Go from there, I believe in you!-vinaigrette: add ingredients to a jar and shake well! Toss with salad ingredients -lay out fries, top with cheese sauce, then steak and garlic aoili. I added parmesan, green onions and pepper to the top!! Put the salad on the side to be healthy…. ENJOYYY!#OnlyPans

item.title: funky stir fry ft red glasses 🕺🏻 Ingredients: -flank steak...
item.description:
View on Instagram


funky stir fry ft red glasses 🕺🏻 Ingredients: -flank steak -broccolini -red bell pepper -white onion -carrot -avocado oil -white rice -sauce: soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, honey, grated garlic cloves, grated ginger, red chili flakes, corn starch Recipe: -chop veggies to your liking :) -mix together sauce ingredients. I measured with my heart but did about equal parts soy sauce and water, 3 garlic cloves, good amount of ginger, 1-2tbsp rice vinegar, and honey to taste, dash of chili flakes, 1tbsp ish corn starch…. I believe in you !!! -add oil to a pan on med heat. Add veggies, season with salt and cook until tender and starts to get some color (5ish min). Remove from pan -add steak and cook for 1-2min each side add veggies back and cover with toss. Toss until everything is fully coated and sauce is shiny *fish eye clips filmed on my @gopro hero 13 using max lens!

item.title: Candied pecans!! Recipe below :) These nuts are making me crazzz...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Candied pecans!! Recipe below :) These nuts are making me crazzzyyy Ingredients: -2 cups pecans -1/4cup brown sugar -1 1/2tsp cinnamon -1/2tsp vanilla extract (or more loll) -1/2tsp salt -dash of nutmeg and black pepper -1 1/2tbsp water Recipe: -in a cold pan add all the ingredients (besides the pecans). Turn heat slightly below medium. Stir everything together until melted and it starts to become frothy (1-2minutes) -add pecans and mix until fully coated, shiny, and have a nutty smell. 3-5 minutes. The liquid will also be significantly reduced, this is when I remove them! Be careful not to burn them! -add to a nonstick surface (parchment paper or silicone mat). Let cool and taste-if you want them to be harder in the outside put them in the oven at 325F for 5-10 min. (I usually do 8 minutes and it’s perfect!!) The outsides will get bubbly and harden as it cools -wait to eat until fully cooled. Store in airtight container for 1 week :) *recipe adapted from Inspired Taste !!
